steps.title: Identify the clause structure and what's needed
steps.content: After the blank is the phrase “a 'power wall' in which more heat was produced than could be efficiently dissipated led developers to invent the dual-core chip in the early 2000s.” This sentence contains the verb “led,” which is the main verb of an independent clause. An independent clause requires a grammatical subject.

steps.title: Determine which choice provides the required subject
steps.content: A gerund phrase can serve as the subject of an independent clause. Option A provides the gerund phrase “Hitting a 'power wall'…” as the subject for “led.” Options B, C, and D each fail to provide correct grammar—B creates a dependent clause without a proper main clause, C creates two independent clauses without proper joining, and D leaves the verb “led” without a subject. That matches choice A — A is correct.
